verdict.summary: Publisher and subscriber use different message types on the same topic name.

dead_ends.action: Change one side's message type without updating the other
dead_ends.why_fails: Causes the same error on the other node; both must agree on the exact message type

dead_ends.action: Use a generic Any type or raw bytes
dead_ends.why_fails: ROS 2 does not support untyped topics; DDS requires concrete IDL types for serialization

workarounds.action: Check actual topic type with ros2 topic info
workarounds.how: ros2 topic info /cmd_vel --verbose  # shows type for each publisher/subscriber

workarounds.action: Ensure both nodes import the same message package and type
workarounds.how: from geometry_msgs.msg import Twist  # must match on both sides

workarounds.action: Use ros2 interface show to inspect the message definition

frequently_confused_with.distinction: QoS incompatibility: same type but different delivery guarantees. Type mismatch: different message types on same topic name.
